Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_HEALTH_TEMPSALOXY_BALTIC_mbi_sto2tz_gotland. 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
This document provides to Copernicus Marine Service users information on the quality 
of the time -depth plots of salinity, temperature and dissolved oxygen in the Gotland 
Basin. These quantities were chosen to represent the inflow events of the Baltic Sea and 
therefore grouped as a separate Copernicus Marine Service product which is named 
BALTIC_OMI_WMHE_mbi_sto2tz_gotland.  
The time-depth data of the salinity, temperature and dissolved oxygen are extracted 
from INSITU_BAL_PHYBGCWAV_DISCRETE_MYNRT_013_032 product. 
c) Copernicus Marine products used as input for the computation 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
INSITU_BAL_PHYBGCWAV_DISCR
ETE_MYNRT_013_032 (insitu 
observations)

2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Baltic Sea Major Baltic Inflow: time/depth evolution S,T,O2  
The accuracy of the index is directly dependent on the accuracy of observation data 
(product INSITU_BAL_PHYBGCWAV_DISCRETE_MYNRT_013_032). The index is constructed 
directly from CTD data at the Gotland deep monitoring station. According to the QUID 
of INSITU_BAL_PHYBGCWAV_DISCRETE_MYNRT_013_032 the accuracy of CTD 
temperature, salinity and oxygen observations are 0.005 – 0.001 ˚C, 0.02 – 0.003 and 8 
mMol, respectively (Figure 1).  
Scientific evaluation can be found in the Copernicus Marine Service Ocean State 
Report (Raudsepp et al., 2

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the quality of time-depth plots for salinity, temperature, and dissolved oxygen in the Gotland Basin of the Baltic Sea, using data from the INSITU_BAL_PHYBGCWAV_DISCRETE_MYNRT_013_032 product.
